scenario: At Helios Healthcare, clinicians work through 1500 tasks per day in Tasklist on Camunda 8.8. A new clinician opens the Tasks page for the first time and wants to understand which screen region holds the queue and filters and which region shows the selected task contents.
question: How is the Tasks page laid out in Tasklist for Camunda 8?

options.text: The left side of the page shows task filters and the queue of tasks, and the right side shows the details of the currently selected task in the queue
The left side shows the BPMN process diagram for navigation, and the right side shows the queue of tasks plus the details of the currently selected task in the queue
The top of the page shows the queue of tasks as a horizontal strip, and the bottom area shows the details and forms for the currently selected task in the queue
The left side shows only tasks assigned to the current user, and the right side shows a separate read-only feed of unassigned tasks still waiting in the shared queue

a.text: Correct. The Tasks page is split into a left pane that holds task filters and the queue of available tasks, and a right pane that shows the selected task details.

b.text: Incorrect. The Tasks page does not embed the BPMN diagram in its main layout; the diagram is reached from the Process tab inside an opened task detail view instead.

c.text: Incorrect. The Tasks page uses a side-by-side queue and detail layout, not a top strip with a bottom pane; that arrangement does not match the documented page structure.

d.text: Incorrect. The Tasks page does not split into separate assigned and unassigned feeds; the filters in the left pane control which subset of tasks the single queue shows.

explanation: The Tasks page in Tasklist shows task filters and the queue on the left side and the details of the currently selected task on the right side, forming a side-by-side layout.
